English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Hiram Corson papers
Corson, Hiram, 1828-1911.
Colman, Gould P. (Gould Patchin), 1926-
Sjoegren, Signe.
14-12-449
4.8 cubic feet. 4.8 cubic feet.
1842-1956. Thirty scrapbooks containing letters, clippings, photographs, and printed materials relating to Corson's travels, life, and career; correspondence exchanged between Corson and colleagues on Shakespeare, Chaucer, Tennyson, Browning, and other figures of English literature; reviews of books and articles by Corson and other scholars; announcements, programs, letters, notes, and reviews pertaining to Corson's activities as public lecturer and reader; letters from friends and family members dealing chiefly with personal matters: health, progress of career, daily activities; one scrapbook devoted to spiritualism, including letters from and about Madame Helena Petrovna Blavatsky, co-founder of the American Theosophical Society. Unbound material includes large numbers of family letters with extensive comments on the early days of Cornell University and life in Ithaca, New York, accounts of the travels of Corson, his wife, Caroline, and their son Eugene, their daily work and activities; clippings, pamphlets, reprints of articles relating to Corson's work in the field of English literature and that of his son in medicine; drafts of lectures and articles; anatomical, physiological, and surgical drawings; photographs; a syllabus; and miscellaneous books. Also, a tape recorded interview with Signe Sjoegren conducted by Gould P. Colman concerning the activities of Corson during the last seven years of his life. Correspondents include Louise Andrews, Matthew Arnold, Henry Ward Beecher, Madame Blavatsky, Edwin Booth, Robert Browning, Francis James Child, Mary Cowden Clarke, George William Curtis, Edward Everett, Willard Fiske, Jessie Fothergill, Horace Howard Furness, Frederick J. Furnivall, Daniel Coit Gilman, Samuel Stehman Haldeman, Edward Everett Hale, James Orchard Halliwell-Phillipps, Bret Harte, Nathaniel Hawthorne, Oliver Wendell Holmes, William Dean Howells, Clement Mansfield Ingleby, William James, Pierre Janet, Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, James Russell Lowell, John M. D. Meiklejohn, J. Parker Norris, Goldwin Smith, Andrew D. White, Walt Whitman, John Greenleaf Whittier, and Woodrow Wilson. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 2 3 ======= 1 2 3 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2082596
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Fredrika Loew, Laura Linke
Date completed:
February 2018
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, February 2018
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, September 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Thirty scrapbooks containing letters, clippings, photographs, and printed materials relating to Corson's travels, life, and career; correspondence exchanged between Corson and colleagues on Shakespeare, Chaucer, Tennyson, Browning, and other figures of English literature; reviews of books and articles by Corson and other scholars; announcements, programs, letters, notes, and reviews pertaining to Corson's activities as public lecturer and reader; letters from friends and family members dealing chiefly with personal matters: health, progress of career, daily activities; one scrapbook devoted to spiritualism, including letters from and about Madame Helena Petrovna Blavatsky, co-founder of the American Theosophical Society. Unbound material includes large numbers of family letters with extensive comments on the early days of Cornell University and life in Ithaca, New York, accounts of the travels of Corson, his wife, Caroline, and their son Eugene, their daily work and activities; clippings, pamphlets, reprints of articles relating to Corson's work in the field of English literature and that of his son in medicine; drafts of lectures and articles; anatomical, physiological, and surgical drawings; photographs; a syllabus; and miscellaneous books. Also, a tape recorded interview with Signe Sjoegren conducted by Gould P. Colman concerning the activities of Corson during the last seven years of his life.
Correspondents include Louise Andrews, Matthew Arnold, Henry Ward Beecher, Madame Blavatsky, Edwin Booth, Robert Browning, Francis James Child, Mary Cowden Clarke, George William Curtis, Edward Everett, Willard Fiske, Jessie Fothergill, Horace Howard Furness, Frederick J. Furnivall, Daniel Coit Gilman, Samuel Stehman Haldeman, Edward Everett Hale, James Orchard Halliwell-Phillipps, Bret Harte, Nathaniel Hawthorne, Oliver Wendell Holmes, William Dean Howells, Clement Mansfield Ingleby, William James, Pierre Janet, Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, James Russell Lowell, John M. D. Meiklejohn, J. Parker Norris, Goldwin Smith, Andrew D. White, Walt Whitman, John Greenleaf Whittier, and Woodrow Wilson.
RELATED MATERIALS
Related collection:
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence
Series II. Spiritualism Materials
Series III. General Documents
Series IV. Eugene Rollins Corson material
Series V. Pauline Corson material
Series VI. Photographs
Cite As:
Hiram Corson papers, #14-12-449. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Professor of English literature. Hiram Corson taught literature courses at Girard College, St. Johns College in Annapolis, Maryland, and at Cornell University.
Photographs. Drawings. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Description and travel. College teachers. Women mystics. Spiritualism. Medicine. English literature. Authors. Andrews, Louise. Arnold, Matthew, 1822-1888. Beecher, Henry Ward, 1813-1887. Blavatsky, H. P. (Helena Petrovna), 1831-1891. Booth, Edwin, 1833-1893. Browning, Robert, 1812-1889. Browning, Robert, 1812-1889 -- : Criticism and interpretation. Chaucer, Geoffrey, -1400 -- : Criticism and interpretation. Child, Francis James, 1825-1896. Clarke, Mary Cowden, 1809-1898. Corson, Caroline. Corson, Eugene. Curtis, George William, 1824-1892. Everett, Edward. Fiske, Willard. Fothergill, Jessie, 1851-1891. Furness, Horace Howard, 1833-1912. Furnivall, Frederick James, 1825-1910. Gillman, Daniel Coit. Haldeman, Samuel Stehman, 1812-1880. Hale, Edward Everett, 1822-1909. Halliwell-Phillipps, J. O. (James Orchard), 1820-1889. Harte, Bret, 1836-1902 Hawthorne, Nathaniel, 1804-1864. Holmes, Oliver Wendell, 1809-1894. Howells, William Dean, 1837-1920. Ingleby, C. M. (Clement Mansfield), 1823-1886. James, William, 1842-1910. Janet, Pierre, 1859-1947. Longfellow, Henry Wadsworth, 1807-1882. Lowell, James Russell, 1819-1891. Meiklejohn, John M. D. Norris, Joseph Parker, 1847-1916. Shakespeare, William, 1564-1616 -- : Criticism and interpretation. Smith, Goldwin, 1823-1910. Tennyson, Alfred Tennyson, Baron, 1809-1892 -- : Criticism and interpretation. White, Andrew Dickson, 1832-1918. Whitman, Walt, 1819-1892. Whittier, John Greenleaf, 1807-1924. Wilson, Woodrow, 1856-1924. Theosophical Society in America Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University -- : History.
2082596 <<<<<<< HEAD Series I. Correspondence
Scope and Contents
Includes letters from Eugene, Pauline, and Caroline Corson. Note: some of the letters were written to Eugene or Mrs. Corson. Many letters refer to spiritualism.Old storage envelopes from correspondenceUndated11CorrespondenceExpression12CorrespondenceIncludes Henry Wadsworth Longfellow letters to Caroline Rollin Corson: March 9, 1855-Regrets his publisher declined the translation on grounds of expense of printing in foreign language. Information about certain poems. Grieved about misfortuen which has 1855-186013Longfellow, Henry WadsworthCorrespondenceIncludes Nathaniel Hawthorne letter, dated February 17, 1964: Hawthorne thanks Corson for gift of Chaucer's "Legende of Goode Women" with hopes expressed for American edition of Chaucer's "Canterbury Pilgrims" edited in the same manner. Also includes some1861-186414Hawthorne, NathanielCorrespondenceIncludes address of Hiram Corson on occasion of his induction as professor at Girard College.1865-186615Everett, EdwardCorrespondenceIncludes James Russell Lowell letter, dated October 24, 1867, to "my dear child" in which he regrets delay in publishing Mr. Corson's work and gladly would double subscription if it would help.1867-186916Lowell, James RussellCorrespondence187017Correspondence187118Correspondence187219Correspondence1873110CorrespondenceIncludes letter from Bret Harte, and letter from Edwin Booth with references to Hamlet.1874-01-1874-05111Harte, BretBooth, EdwinCorrespondenceIncludes printed pamphlets and posters for performances, correspondence on Pauline's death.1874-06-1874-12112Correspondence1875113Correspondence1876-01-1876-09114Correspondence1876-10-1876-12115Correspondence1877-01-1877-05116Correspondence1877-06117Correspondence1877-07118Correspondence1877-08-1877-12119Correspondence1878-01-1878-07120Correspondence1878-08-1878-09121Correspondence1878-10-1878-11122Correspondence1878-12123Correspondence1879-01-1879-04124Correspondence1879-05-1879-12125Correspondence1880126CorrespondenceIncludes two letters from Robert Browning: one inviting the Corsons' to lunch and another setting a convenient time to call.1881-1882127Browning, RobertCorrespondence1883128Correspondence1884129Correspondence1885130CorrespondenceIncludes a letter from Robert Browning expressing thanks and letter from Walt Whitman sending notes and mentioning Woodruff's visit. Also includes letter from Edward Everett Hale.1886131Browning, RobertHale, Edward EverettWhitman, WaltCorrespondenceIncludes land deed and letter from Gabrielle DeVeaux Clements regarding Corson's portrait.1887-1888132Clements, Gabrielle DeVeauxGosse, EdmundCowden Clarke, Mary VictoriaCorrespondenceIncludes photograph of Shakespeare's gloves.1889133Cowden Clarke, Mary VictoriaCorrespondence1890-1891134Cowden Clarke, Mary VictoriaCorrespondence1892-01-1892-0921Tolstoy, Sophie AndreevnaCorrespondence1892-10-1892-1222Correspondence1893-189423Correspondence1895-01-1895-0824Correspondence1895-09-1895-1225CorrespondenceIncludes letter from Prince Serge Wolkonsky.1896-189726Wolkonsky, SergeCorrespondenceIncludes letter and poems by Gottfried Hult.189827Hult, GottfriedCorrespondence1899-190028CorrespondenceIncludes letter to Caroline Corson from Dr. Silas Weir Mitchell.1901-01-1901-0529Correspondence1901-05210Correspondence1901-06-1901-07211Correspondence1901-08-1901-12212Correspondence1902-01-1902-08213Correspondence1902-09-1902-12214Correspondence1903-01-1903-02215Correspondence1903-03-1903-05216CorrespondenceIncludes letter, dated November 18, 1903, from Woodrow Wilson congratulation Corson.1903-06-1903-12217Mitchell, Silas WeirHaultain, Theodore ArnoldWilson, WoodrowCorrespondence1904-01-1904-05218Wier, Silas MitchellCorrespondence1904-06-1904-12219Correspondence1905-01-1905-04220Correspondence1905-05-1905-08221Correspondence1905-09-1905-12222CorrespondenceIncludes letters from Hiram Corson to Eugene Corson regarding Robert Browning: "On Wednesday evening, I read to my spiritual visitors my reminiscences of Browning, who had asked for them at the previous sitting [i. e., seance]. There were present Ma, Paul1906-01-1906-03223Correspondence1906-04-1906-09224Correspondence1906-10-1906-12225Stoneman, BerthaCorrespondence1907-01-1907-04226Correspondence1907-05-1907-12227Correspondence1908-01-1908-07228Correspondence1908-08-1908-12229Besant, AnnieCorrespondence1909-01-1909-07230CorrespondenceIncludes last will and testament of Hiram Corson1909-08-1909-12231Correspondence1910232Correspondence1911-01-1911-07233Correspondence1911-0831Correspondence1911-09-1911-1232Correspondence191233Correspondence1913-191534Correspondence1920-193134CorrespondenceIncludes newspaper clippings and receipts.1932-195635Sjoegren, SigneCorrespondenceIncludes letter from Fannie Barrett Browning postponing an engagement with Mrs. Corson on account of husband's headache. Also contains newspaper clippings.Undated36Browning, Fannie BarrettMcGraw Fiske, JennieOsler, WilliamHorace Howard Furnace letters to Hiram Corson1872-10-10-1909-06-0238Letters from Joseph Henry1850-187139Letters from F.J. Childs and George Allen1867-1881310Letters and clippings from H.R. Haweis1893311Series II. Spiritualism MaterialsLetters from Louisa Andrews to the CorsonsLetters concerning spiritualism, merits of various mediums, spread of faith, and Olcott-Blavatsky partnership. Also includes letter to Andrews from JM Roberts calling into question the motives of Helena Petrovna Blavatsky and Henry Steel Olcott.1874-11-1876-0237Letters from Helena Petrovna BlavatskyWritten by Blavatsky to Mrs. Caroline Corson (written in French and referring to Louisa Andrews), Alexander Akozokof introducing Mrs. Corson, and photostat of letter to Hiram.1875-1878312Letter to Madame Blavatsky from J. R. BuchananConcerning articles on science and religion.1875-08-29313Letter to Madame Blavatsky from Francis F. LippittConcerning the authenticity of the "materializations" in which Mr. and Mrs. Holmes and Dr. Child were involved; mentions the "deep disgrace" of Katie King; also refers to Professor Corson, Mrs. Andrews of Springfield, and Colonel Olcott.1875-03-03313Letter to Madame Blavatsky from S. P. StephensConcerning alleged spiritualist frauds of Henry T. Child. Refers to a couple named Holmes, to a Katie King, and a Mrs. White. Also signed by Francis J. Lippitt as witness1875-01-30313Letters to Henry Steel Olcott from Emma H. Britten and J. M. Peebles1875314Letter to Hiram Corson from G.L. Ditson1875-10-22315Letters to Hiram Corson from V.A. Taylor1876-01-12315Letter to Hiram Corson from Luther ColbyLuther Colby, editor of the Banner of Light, accepting for publication an article by Corsonattacking the American Theosophical Society1876-01-02315Letters to Hiram Corson from Emilie G. JonesEmilie Jones, sister of Louisa Andrews, reporting on latter's health.1875315Statement of William H. Westcott, concerning the seances of Mr. and Mrs. Holmes; witnessed by Francis J. Lippitt.1875-01-31315Letter from David Higham to Eugene R. Corson regarding Blavatsky photographs1929-07-19316Clippings and publications on spiritualism and Helena Blavatsky1870-1890317Photograph of Helena Blavatsky by Beardsley in Ithaca187555Series III. Scope and Contents
Formerly on display in the A.D. White library ======= Series I. Correspondence
Scope and Contents
Includes letters from Eugene, Pauline, and Caroline Corson. Note: some of the letters were written to Eugene or Mrs. Corson. Many letters refer to spiritualism.Old storage envelopes from correspondenceUndated11CorrespondenceExpression12CorrespondenceIncludes Henry Wadsworth Longfellow letters to Caroline Rollin Corson: March 9, 1855-Regrets his publisher declined the translation on grounds of expense of printing in foreign language. Information about certain poems. Grieved about misfortuen which has 1855-186013Longfellow, Henry WadsworthCorrespondenceIncludes Nathaniel Hawthorne letter, dated February 17, 1964: Hawthorne thanks Corson for gift of Chaucer's "Legende of Goode Women" with hopes expressed for American edition of Chaucer's "Canterbury Pilgrims" edited in the same manner.
